title: mentha longifolia var. balsamiflora
intro: Mentha longifolia var. balsamiflora, also known as Horsemint or Balsam Mint, is a perennial plant in the mint family, Lamiaceae, characterized by its long stems and fragrant, balsam-like leaves. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, including its essential oils, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, headaches, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Horsemint include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and calm the body, while also providing relief from symptoms of colds and flu. Horsemint can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
